questions.workflow_description: Screens inbound leads against ICP rules, source quality, budget/timeline clues, and response urgency so sales only chases leads worth fast follow-up.
questions.workflow_objective: Higher lead-to-opportunity conversion and faster speed-to-lead
questions.question_text: Lead Qualification Assistant — Business Value
questions.guidance: Whether the use case moves a meaningful commercial or operational KPI rather than saving only trivial effort.
questions.evidence_examples: KPI link, affected team size, customer or cash impact, cost of delay, and volume of affected work
questions.customer_impact_if_weak: Sales teams spend too much time reading weak leads manually and good leads wait too long for first response.
questions.score_1_guidance: Solves a minor convenience issue with no KPI or customer impact.
questions.score_3_guidance: Improves a relevant workflow metric but the commercial upside is still moderate or indirect.
questions.score_5_guidance: Directly affects a top business KPI such as revenue conversion, retention, margin protection, cash collection, or service reliability.

questions.workflow_description: Screens inbound leads against ICP rules, source quality, budget/timeline clues, and response urgency so sales only chases leads worth fast follow-up.
questions.workflow_objective: Higher lead-to-opportunity conversion and faster speed-to-lead
questions.question_text: Lead Qualification Assistant — Feasibility
questions.guidance: Whether the workflow is stable enough and the operating model clear enough to implement without redesigning the business first.
questions.evidence_examples: Workflow documentation, owner clarity, exception rate, SOP maturity, and pilot control design
questions.customer_impact_if_weak: Sales teams spend too much time reading weak leads manually and good leads wait too long for first response.
questions.score_1_guidance: Workflow is unstable, undocumented, or ownership is unclear.
questions.score_3_guidance: Workflow is partly defined and could support a narrow pilot with active manual oversight.
questions.score_5_guidance: Workflow is already disciplined, repeatable, and implementation can happen without major redesign.

questions.workflow_description: Screens inbound leads against ICP rules, source quality, budget/timeline clues, and response urgency so sales only chases leads worth fast follow-up.
questions.workflow_objective: Higher lead-to-opportunity conversion and faster speed-to-lead
questions.question_text: Lead Qualification Assistant — Data Availability
questions.guidance: Whether the inputs needed for the use case actually exist, are accessible, and are good enough to trust outputs.
questions.evidence_examples: Input fields, document access, transcript quality, source-of-truth confidence, and data ownership
questions.customer_impact_if_weak: Sales teams spend too much time reading weak leads manually and good leads wait too long for first response.
questions.score_1_guidance: Required data is missing, inaccessible, or too poor to support the use case.
questions.score_3_guidance: Enough data exists for a scoped pilot, but cleanup or supplementation is still needed.
questions.score_5_guidance: Required data is already captured cleanly, consistently, and at enough history depth for the use case.

questions.workflow_description: Screens inbound leads against ICP rules, source quality, budget/timeline clues, and response urgency so sales only chases leads worth fast follow-up.
questions.workflow_objective: Higher lead-to-opportunity conversion and faster speed-to-lead
questions.question_text: Lead Qualification Assistant — Integration Ease
questions.guidance: How easily the output can be embedded into the workflow or system where teams already work.
questions.evidence_examples: API/connectors, handoff points, system of record, queue insertion, and technical owner availability
questions.customer_impact_if_weak: Sales teams spend too much time reading weak leads manually and good leads wait too long for first response.
questions.score_1_guidance: No practical insertion point exists; output would stay outside the workflow.
questions.score_3_guidance: A pilot can run with some manual handling, but workflow embedding is still partial.
questions.score_5_guidance: Output can be embedded directly into the operational workflow with low technical friction.

questions.workflow_description: Screens inbound leads against ICP rules, source quality, budget/timeline clues, and response urgency so sales only chases leads worth fast follow-up.
questions.workflow_objective: Higher lead-to-opportunity conversion and faster speed-to-lead
questions.question_text: Lead Qualification Assistant — Risk Level
questions.guidance: How safely the use case can be piloted given data sensitivity, customer exposure, and error consequences. Higher score = lower risk.
questions.evidence_examples: Data sensitivity, customer-facing exposure, compliance impact, reversibility, and review requirement
questions.customer_impact_if_weak: Sales teams spend too much time reading weak leads manually and good leads wait too long for first response.
questions.score_1_guidance: High-risk use case touching sensitive data or critical external decisions with weak controls.
questions.score_3_guidance: Moderate risk; usable in pilot with defined controls and human review.
questions.score_5_guidance: Low-risk use case with contained consequences, strong guardrails, and easy human override.

usecases.usecase_description: Screens inbound leads against ICP rules, source quality, budget/timeline clues, and response urgency so sales only chases leads worth fast follow-up.
usecases.workflow_trigger: New form fill, website enquiry, WhatsApp lead, or SDR handoff
usecases.operational_pain_point: Sales teams spend too much time reading weak leads manually and good leads wait too long for first response.
usecases.main_input_data: Lead form fields, source campaign, website notes, prior contact history
usecases.output_or_action: Priority score, qualification summary, next-step recommendation, and owner routing
usecases.integration_point: CRM lead record and assignment workflow
usecases.business_value_driver: Higher lead-to-opportunity conversion and faster speed-to-lead
usecases.core_success_metric: Qualified lead rate and median first-response time
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Lead source fields, ICP rules, and routing owners must already be defined

usecases.usecase_description: Creates first-draft outreach emails or LinkedIn messages using account context, industry pain points, and recent buyer signals.
usecases.operational_pain_point: Reps lose time writing repetitive first-touch messages and quality varies heavily by seller.
usecases.main_input_data: CRM account data, website summary, campaign angle, rep notes
usecases.output_or_action: Personalized outreach draft with subject line, opening hook, and CTA
usecases.integration_point: CRM, sales engagement platform, or email draft workflow
usecases.business_value_driver: More meetings booked per rep hour and stronger outbound consistency
usecases.core_success_metric: Meeting-booked rate from outbound and draft acceptance/edit ratio
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Core account/contact data and approved messaging guardrails must exist

usecases.usecase_description: Builds first-draft proposals, statements of work, or scope summaries from discovery notes, pricing inputs, and standard service components.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Opportunity enters proposal stage or scope revision requested
usecases.operational_pain_point: Proposal turnaround is slow, senior staff rewrite similar scopes repeatedly, and pricing context gets lost between teams.
usecases.main_input_data: Discovery notes, approved service catalogue, pricing assumptions, case studies, templates
usecases.output_or_action: Proposal draft, scope summary, assumptions list, and missing-input prompts
usecases.integration_point: Proposal template, document workspace, and CRM opportunity record
usecases.business_value_driver: Faster proposal turnaround with less senior delivery time consumed
usecases.core_success_metric: Proposal cycle time and win-rate on proposals delivered within SLA
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Approved proposal templates, service catalogue, and pricing logic must be current

usecases.usecase_name: Meeting Summary and Action Capture Assistant
usecases.usecase_description: Summarizes internal or client meetings into decisions, owners, risks, and next actions that can be pushed into task workflows.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Meeting transcript or notes become available
usecases.operational_pain_point: Teams lose actions after meetings, notes are inconsistent, and follow-up depends on whoever took the best notes.
usecases.main_input_data: Meeting transcript, calendar metadata, agenda, manual notes
usecases.output_or_action: Decision summary, action list, owner assignments, and unresolved-question log
usecases.integration_point: Meeting platform, project tool, or task tracker
usecases.business_value_driver: Less follow-up leakage and faster execution after meetings
usecases.core_success_metric: Action completion rate and time from meeting end to action circulation
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Meeting recording/transcript access and action-owner conventions must exist

usecases.usecase_name: Support Ticket Triage and Routing Assistant
usecases.usecase_description: Reads new support requests, classifies issue type and urgency, and routes the case to the correct queue with a recommended first response.
usecases.workflow_trigger: New email, portal ticket, or chat escalation is created
usecases.operational_pain_point: Support teams waste time manually sorting tickets, urgent issues are missed, and queue quality depends on agent experience.
usecases.main_input_data: Ticket text, customer tier, product/service context, prior case history
usecases.output_or_action: Issue category, urgency flag, queue routing, and suggested acknowledgement draft
usecases.integration_point: Helpdesk queue, inbox routing rules, or CRM case module
usecases.business_value_driver: Lower first-response delays and better queue quality
usecases.core_success_metric: First-response SLA attainment and misroute rate
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Ticket categories, escalation rules, and queue ownership must already be defined

usecases.usecase_description: Answers internal staff questions using approved SOPs, policy documents, service playbooks, and common issue resolutions.
usecases.workflow_trigger: User asks a policy, SOP, or process question
usecases.operational_pain_point: Staff rely on tribal knowledge, search across scattered files, or interrupt senior team members for routine answers.
usecases.main_input_data: Approved SOPs, FAQs, policy docs, service manuals, templates
usecases.output_or_action: Contextual answer with cited source document snippets and escalation cue if confidence is low
usecases.integration_point: Chat interface, intranet, or internal help workflow
usecases.business_value_driver: Less interruption of senior staff and faster task execution
usecases.core_success_metric: Question resolution time and reduction in repeat internal queries
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Source documents must be current, approved, and access-controlled

usecases.usecase_description: Checks onboarding packs for missing documents, inconsistent fields, or obvious handoff risks before the team moves the client live.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Customer submits onboarding forms or compliance documents
usecases.operational_pain_point: Onboarding teams manually check packs line by line, miss missing items, and discover problems late in the process.
usecases.main_input_data: Application forms, contracts, ID/compliance docs, implementation checklist
usecases.output_or_action: Missing-item checklist, inconsistency flags, and readiness status suggestion
usecases.integration_point: Client onboarding checklist and case workflow
usecases.business_value_driver: Shorter onboarding cycle time and fewer late-stage rework loops
usecases.core_success_metric: First-pass completeness rate and onboarding cycle time
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Document checklist, acceptance rules, and human approval steps must be clear

usecases.usecase_name: Invoice and Remittance Query Classification Assistant
usecases.usecase_description: Classifies inbound accounts-receivable emails into dispute, remittance, proof-of-delivery, copy-invoice, or payment-delay types and suggests the right next queue.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Customer finance email arrives in shared AR inbox
usecases.operational_pain_point: Finance staff spend time manually reading repetitive emails and urgent cash-impacting issues are not surfaced early enough.
usecases.main_input_data: Inbound email, invoice reference, account status, prior dispute history
usecases.output_or_action: Query type, priority flag, next-owner queue, and response guidance
usecases.integration_point: Shared finance inbox, AR case board, or ERP notes
usecases.business_value_driver: Faster cash-resolution workflow and reduced inbox backlog
usecases.core_success_metric: Inbox aging and average resolution time for payment-related queries
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Common query categories and escalation rules must already be agreed

usecases.usecase_description: Ranks overdue accounts by likelihood of recovery, customer risk, and urgency so teams focus attention where cash impact is highest.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Daily or weekly overdue debtor review
usecases.operational_pain_point: Collectors often work the debtor list oldest-first instead of highest-impact-first, leaving easy cash and high-risk debt unattended.
usecases.main_input_data: Aging data, payment history, dispute status, customer tier, promise-to-pay notes
usecases.output_or_action: Priority queue, recovery rationale, and recommended follow-up style
usecases.integration_point: AR aging report, collector worklist, or CRM notes
usecases.business_value_driver: Better cash collection focus and lower aged debt buildup
usecases.core_success_metric: Cash collected from prioritized accounts and reduction in >90-day debt
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Clean aging data and collector action history must exist

usecases.usecase_description: Turns pipeline, bookings, revenue, margin, or operational KPI movements into leadership-ready commentary that explains what changed and why it matters.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Weekly or monthly reporting cycle begins
usecases.operational_pain_point: Analysts spend hours rewriting commentary that repeats similar themes, while leadership still wants a clear narrative not just numbers.
usecases.main_input_data: KPI tables, pipeline data, budget vs actuals, prior period comparisons
usecases.output_or_action: Narrative summary, variance commentary, and follow-up questions for review
usecases.integration_point: Reporting pack, BI output, or board update draft
usecases.business_value_driver: Faster management reporting with more consistent interpretation of variances
usecases.core_success_metric: Reporting cycle time and percentage of commentary accepted with minor edits
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Baseline KPI definitions and clean source reports must already exist

usecases.usecase_name: Sales Call Review and Deal Risk Assistant
usecases.usecase_description: Reviews sales-call transcripts to surface buying signals, objections, competitor mentions, missing next steps, and deal-risk themes for coaching.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Sales call recording or note capture completed
usecases.operational_pain_point: Managers cannot review every call, coaching quality is uneven, and deal risk often shows up too late in pipeline review.
usecases.main_input_data: Call transcript, opportunity stage, CRM notes, competitor list
usecases.output_or_action: Coaching summary, deal-risk note, objection themes, and next-step gap flag
usecases.integration_point: Conversation-intelligence tool or CRM opportunity notes
usecases.business_value_driver: Improved deal inspection quality and better manager coaching leverage
usecases.core_success_metric: Manager review coverage and late-stage deal slippage rate
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Call recording/transcription and stage definitions must exist

usecases.usecase_name: Renewal and Churn Risk Review Assistant
usecases.usecase_description: Reviews customer signals to flag renewals at risk, summarize likely causes, and prompt the success team on the best intervention path.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Monthly renewal review or trigger event on customer account
usecases.operational_pain_point: Success teams often find risk too late and account reviews depend on whoever knows the customer best.
usecases.main_input_data: Usage trends, support history, NPS/feedback, invoice/payment signals, renewal date
usecases.output_or_action: Risk summary, likely churn drivers, and recommended intervention type
usecases.integration_point: CSM account review, CRM renewal board, or customer health dashboard
usecases.business_value_driver: Earlier intervention on risky renewals and better retention focus
usecases.core_success_metric: Renewal save rate and percentage of at-risk accounts identified 30+ days early
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Customer health indicators and renewal ownership model must exist

usecases.usecase_description: Guides staff through approved internal processes such as leave, procurement, incident logging, or service handoffs using current policy language.
usecases.workflow_trigger: User asks how to complete a governed internal task
usecases.operational_pain_point: Teams interpret policies differently, compliance steps get skipped, and managers answer the same questions repeatedly.
usecases.main_input_data: Approved policy documents, SOPs, checklists, forms, approval matrix
usecases.output_or_action: Step-by-step guidance, required documents, and escalation point if exception applies
usecases.integration_point: Internal portal, HR helpdesk, or workflow launcher
usecases.business_value_driver: Fewer policy deviations and faster completion of governed tasks
usecases.core_success_metric: Policy-related query resolution time and reduction in process rework
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Policies must be approved, current, and version-controlled

usecases.usecase_description: Flags orders, jobs, or service cases that break expected patterns such as missing fields, unusual delays, repeated rework, or breached handoffs.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Scheduled review of operational pipeline or live transaction event
usecases.operational_pain_point: Operational issues are noticed only after a customer complains or a manager manually spots a pattern in spreadsheets.
usecases.main_input_data: Order/job data, timestamps, status history, handoff logs, exception codes
usecases.output_or_action: Exception alert, likely root-cause hypothesis, and recommended follow-up owner
usecases.integration_point: Ops dashboard, workflow queue, or service board
usecases.business_value_driver: Fewer silent failures and quicker intervention on service leakage
usecases.core_success_metric: Exception resolution time and reduction in reopened/reworked cases
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Workflow timestamps and status definitions must be reliable

usecases.usecase_description: Builds a concise executive narrative from multiple functional updates so leadership sees the operating story, not only disconnected department notes.
usecases.workflow_trigger: Weekly exco pack or monthly ops review is assembled
usecases.operational_pain_point: Leaders receive disconnected updates, report assembly is manual, and the same consolidation work is repeated every cycle.
usecases.main_input_data: Department updates, KPI dashboards, issue logs, project status notes
usecases.output_or_action: Executive summary, key decisions required, top risks, and cross-functional dependencies
usecases.integration_point: Leadership pack, board memo, or operating review workflow
usecases.business_value_driver: Less reporting friction and faster leadership decision-making
usecases.core_success_metric: Time to produce exco pack and number of report revision cycles
usecases.readiness_prerequisite: Department update cadence and KPI source pack must already exist

factors.what_this_factor_tests: Whether the use case moves a meaningful commercial or operational KPI rather than saving only trivial effort.
factors.tailored_scoring_1_desc: Solves a minor convenience issue with no KPI or customer impact.
factors.tailored_scoring_2_desc: Creates a local team benefit only; impact is limited or hard to notice in reporting.
factors.tailored_scoring_3_desc: Improves a relevant workflow metric but the commercial upside is still moderate or indirect.
factors.tailored_scoring_4_desc: Materially improves a departmental KPI such as conversion, SLA, turnaround time, or reporting cycle time.
factors.tailored_scoring_5_desc: Directly affects a top business KPI such as revenue conversion, retention, margin protection, cash collection, or service reliability.
factors.what_to_check: KPI link, affected team size, customer or cash impact, cost of delay, and volume of affected work

factors.what_this_factor_tests: Whether the workflow is stable enough and the operating model clear enough to implement without redesigning the business first.
factors.tailored_scoring_1_desc: Workflow is unstable, undocumented, or ownership is unclear.
factors.tailored_scoring_2_desc: Use case is understandable but process variation, exceptions, or ownership gaps are still major blockers.
factors.tailored_scoring_3_desc: Workflow is partly defined and could support a narrow pilot with active manual oversight.
factors.tailored_scoring_4_desc: Workflow is mostly stable with known inputs, outputs, and review points.
factors.tailored_scoring_5_desc: Workflow is already disciplined, repeatable, and implementation can happen without major redesign.
factors.what_to_check: Workflow documentation, owner clarity, exception rate, SOP maturity, and pilot control design

factors.what_this_factor_tests: Whether the inputs needed for the use case actually exist, are accessible, and are good enough to trust outputs.
factors.tailored_scoring_1_desc: Required data is missing, inaccessible, or too poor to support the use case.
factors.tailored_scoring_2_desc: Some inputs exist but there are major gaps, manual gathering, or poor quality problems.
factors.tailored_scoring_3_desc: Enough data exists for a scoped pilot, but cleanup or supplementation is still needed.
factors.tailored_scoring_4_desc: Most required data is available, accessible, and reasonably reliable.
factors.tailored_scoring_5_desc: Required data is already captured cleanly, consistently, and at enough history depth for the use case.
factors.what_to_check: Input fields, document access, transcript quality, source-of-truth confidence, and data ownership

factors.what_this_factor_tests: How easily the output can be embedded into the workflow or system where teams already work.
factors.tailored_scoring_1_desc: No practical insertion point exists; output would stay outside the workflow.
factors.tailored_scoring_2_desc: Embedding is possible only through heavy manual copy-paste or workaround steps.
factors.tailored_scoring_3_desc: A pilot can run with some manual handling, but workflow embedding is still partial.
factors.tailored_scoring_4_desc: There is a workable integration or handoff path into the user's existing system.
factors.tailored_scoring_5_desc: Output can be embedded directly into the operational workflow with low technical friction.
factors.what_to_check: API/connectors, handoff points, system of record, queue insertion, and technical owner availability

factors.what_this_factor_tests: How safely the use case can be piloted given data sensitivity, customer exposure, and error consequences. Higher score = lower risk.
factors.tailored_scoring_1_desc: High-risk use case touching sensitive data or critical external decisions with weak controls.
factors.tailored_scoring_2_desc: Material risk remains and would require strong manual review on every output.
factors.tailored_scoring_3_desc: Moderate risk; usable in pilot with defined controls and human review.
factors.tailored_scoring_4_desc: Low-to-moderate risk with manageable review requirements and clear rollback options.
factors.tailored_scoring_5_desc: Low-risk use case with contained consequences, strong guardrails, and easy human override.
factors.what_to_check: Data sensitivity, customer-facing exposure, compliance impact, reversibility, and review requirement

factors.what_this_factor_tests: How quickly the business could see a measurable benefit once work starts.
factors.tailored_scoring_1_desc: Would take long preparation with little near-term return.
factors.tailored_scoring_2_desc: Meaningful setup is required before any benefit is visible.
factors.tailored_scoring_3_desc: Pilot benefit could appear in a medium-term window after some foundation work.
factors.tailored_scoring_4_desc: Value should become visible relatively quickly after a controlled pilot starts.
factors.tailored_scoring_5_desc: Fast path to observable benefit because workflow, data, and deployment route are already close to ready.
factors.what_to_check: Implementation lead time, dependencies, stakeholder load, and speed of KPI movement
